                              PLAINTIFFS’ REQUEST FOR DISMISSAL OF CLASS ACTION

        1                      NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION                          
                                                                                    
        2       NOTICE IS HEREBY GIVEN that, on April 16, 2021 at 9:30 a.m., Plaintiffs Dan Beck and
                                                                                    
        3  Bienvenido Salazar (“Plaintiffs”) will move for entry of the proposed Order filed herewith dismissing
                                                                                    
        4  this class action without prejudice, and without notice to putative class members, pursuant to California
                                                                                    
        5  Rules of Court, Rule 3.770.                                              
                                                                                    
        6       This motion is based upon this Notice of Motion and Motion; the Memorandum of Points and
        7  Authorities; the Declaration of Julian Hammond; the proposed Order; and upon such other documentary
                                                                                    
        8  and oral evidence or argument as may be presented to the Court at the hearing for this motion.
                                                                                    
        9                 MEMORANDUM   OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES                    
                                                                                    
       10       I.   BACKGROUND                                                     
                                                                                    
       11       Plaintiffs filed this putative wage and hour class action, pursuant to California Code of Civil
                                                                                    
       12  Procedure § 382, on January 19, 2021, on behalf of themselves and all other similarly situated employees
       13  of Defendant University of San Francisco. The Complaint seeks unpaid wages, unpaid premium pay,
                                                                                    
       14  unreimbursed expenses, restitution, statutory penalties, interest, injunctive and equitable relief, and
                                                                                    
       15  reasonable attorneys’ fees and costs pursuant to California Labor Code §§ 218.5, 226(a), (e), 226.7, 510,
                                                                                    
       16  512, 1194, 2802, Wage Order No. 4-2001, §§ 3, 11, and 12, California Code of Civil Procedure § 1021.5,
                                                                                    
       17  and California Business & Professions Code §§ 17200 et seq. Hammond Decl. ¶ 3.
                                                                                    
       18       The Complaint filed in this action is identical to the Complaint filed in the San Francisco Superior
                                                                                    
       19  Court one week earlier, on January 5, 2021, Case No. CGC-21-588930 (“January 5 Action”). The
       20  Complaint filed in this action is also identical to the Complaint filed in the San Francisco Superior Court
                                                                                    
       21  on January 12, 2021, Case No. CGC-21-589038 (“January 12 Action”). Hammond Decl. ¶¶ 2-3.
                                                                                    
       22       The only reason for the filing of the present action (Case No. CGC-21-589038) and the January
                                                                                    
       23  12 Action (Case No. CGC-21-589038) was because Plaintiffs’ counsel was erroneously advised that the
                                                                                    
       24  January 5 Action (Case No. CGC-21-588930) had been rejected by the Civil Clerk and had not been
                                                                                    
       25  filed. Hammond Decl. ¶ 4.                                                
       26       Neither this action nor the January 12 Action have been served on the Defendant. Plaintiffs are
                                                                                    
       27  seeking dismissal only of the present January 19 Action and, by separate request filed concurrently
                                                                                    
       28  herewith, the January 12 Action. Plaintiffs are not seeking to dismiss their first-filed January 5 Action
                                                                                    
                                             1                                      
    29                                                                              
                              PLAINTIFFS’ REQUEST FOR DISMISSAL OF CLASS ACTION     
    30                                                                              
    31                                                                              
 
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
        1  (which has been served on Defendant). Hammond Decl. ¶¶ 5-7.              
                                                                                    
        2       No class certification motion has been filed in this case, no class has been certified, and notice
                                                                                    
        3  of the pendency of this action has not been provided, nor is required to be provided, to any class
                                                                                    
        4  members. Plaintiffs’ counsel is unaware of any class members who have relied on this action or refrained
                                                                                    
        5  from bringing similar claims due to the existence of this action. Finally, neither Plaintiffs nor Plaintiffs’
                                                                                    
        6  counsel are receiving any consideration, direct or indirect, for dismissing this action. Hammond Decl. ¶
        7  8-9.                                                                     
                                                                                    
        8               II.  PLAINTIFFS’ REQUEST FOR DISMISSAL                      
                                                                                    
        9       In light of Plaintiffs having filed the present action only because Plaintiffs’ counsel was
                                                                                    
       10  erroneously advised that the identical, previously filed, January 5, 2021, action (Case No. CGC-21-
                                                                                    
       11  588930) had been rejected by the Civil Clerk, Plaintiffs respectfully request that the Court dismiss the
                                                                                    
       12  present action without prejudice and without requiring that notice be sent to class members. Hammond
       13  Decl. ¶ 4.                                                               
                                                                                    
       14       Dismissal of this action is appropriate without notice to class members. First, class members will
                                                                                    
       15  not suffer any prejudice as result of the dismissal. California Rules of Court, Rule 3.770(c) states, “if the
                                                                                    
       16  court has not ruled on class certification, or if notice of the pendency of the action has not been provided
                                                                                    
       17  to class members in a case in which such notice was required…the action may be dismissed without
                                                                                    
       18  notice to the class members if the court finds that the dismissal will not prejudice them.” Here, no class
                                                                                    
       19  certification motion has been filed, no class has been certified, and notice of the pendency of this action
       20  has not been provided, nor required to be provided, to any class members. Id. ¶ 8. The dismissal will
                                                                                    
       21  not prejudice class members because the class claims will be dismissed without prejudice. Id. Further,
                                                                                    
       22  Plaintiffs’ counsel is unaware of any class members who have relied on this action, or refrained from
                                                                                    
       23  bringing a similar claim due to the existence of this action. Id. Moreover, class members will have a
                                                                                    
       24  full and fair opportunity to pursue identical claims in the first-filed, January 5, 2021, lawsuit (Case No.
                                                                                    
       25  CGC-21-588930). Id. ¶ 8, 10. Therefore, there will be no prejudice to class members and notice of the
       26  dismissal is not necessary.                                              
                                                                                    
       27       Second, Plaintiffs are not receiving any consideration, direct or indirect for dismissing their
                                                                                    
       28  claims. Hammond Decl. ¶ 9. “[W]henever the dismissal of a class action stems from a defendant’s grant
                                                                                    
                                             2                                      
    29                                                                              
                              PLAINTIFFS’ REQUEST FOR DISMISSAL OF CLASS ACTION     
    30                                                                              
    31                                                                              
 
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
        1  of benefits to the representative plaintiffs, which are not provided to the class as a whole, the court may
                                                                                    
        2  not dismiss the action without notice to the class.” La Sala v. American Savings & Loan Association, 5
                                                                                    
        3  Cal.3d. 864, 868 (1971). In this case, Defendant is not giving any consideration to Plaintiffs or Plaintiffs’
                                                                                    
        4  counsel and this action may be dismissed without notice to class members.
                                                                                    
        5       Therefore, Plaintiffs respectfully request that the Court dismiss this action in its entirety without
                                                                                    
        6  prejudice to Plaintiffs’ claims or to the claims of the putative classes; and without requiring that notice
        7  be given to class members.                                               
                                                                                    
        8                                                                           
                                                                                    
        9                                                                           
            Dated: March 23, 2021      Respectfully submitted,
